description.value:
Humans

Stereotype: Quick to build, quick to die, quick to break their word.

Size: 1M; Speed: 5; Stability: 0

an4b9HdNrni1iSlM.name: Purchased Traits
an4b9HdNrni1iSlM.description: You have 3 ancestry points to spend on the following traits.

description.value: As a maneuver, you can open your awareness to detect supernatural creatures and phenomena. Until the end of your next turn, you know the location of any supernatural object, or any undead, construct, or creature from another world within 5 squares, even if you don't have line of effect. You know if you're detecting an item or a creature, and its nature.
Ignore temporary difficult terrain created by magic/psionics. Reduce forced movement from such sources by 1.
When you take the Help action, the bonus granted is +2 instead of +1.
Triggered action to take half damage from non-untyped damage (fire, cold, etc.).

A chartered frontier company that works the ash-woods and burned margins: disciplined patrols, quotas, and the grim etiquette of boundary law.

Language: Askeskogsmål

Environment: Wilderness; Organization: Bureaucratic; Upbringing: Martial

Skills: Perception, Athletics, Intimidation.

Languages: No additional languages.

Renown: 1; Wealth: 0; Project Points: 50

Perk: *Take the Hit*. Once per encounter, when an ally nearby would suffer the consequences of a hazard or complication, you can step in to take it instead.
Whenever you are in wilderness, once you are hidden from a creature, you don’t need cover or concealment to stay hidden from them.

Strategist. Defender. Leader. With weapon in hand, you lead allies into the maw of battle, barking out commands that inspire your fellow heroes to move faster and strike more precisely. All the while, you stand between your compatriots and death, taunting the followers of evil to best you if they can.

As a tactician, you have abilities that heal your allies and grant them increased damage, movement, and attacks, even as you leave your enemies struggling to respond.

KoGp0Q93aEXIiQxx.name: Out of Position
KoGp0Q93aEXIiQxx.description: Even before battle begins, your enemies struggle to keep up with your tactics.

lffZjjuNWAvfmliT.name: Skills
lffZjjuNWAvfmliT.description: Choose any two skills from Alertness, Architecture, Blacksmithing, Brag, Culture, Empathize, Fletching, Mechanics, Monsters, Search, Strategy, or the skills of the exploration skill group. (Quick Build: Monsters, Strategy.)

TPNKDjRP9rjsZfdo.name: Tactical Doctrine
TPNKDjRP9rjsZfdo.description:
Warfare is as old as civilization—and perhaps even older. As battle became ever more complex, military leaders invented tactical doctrine, outlining how combatants should be structured, used, and deployed. Doctrine can be learned at war colleges passing on ancient martial traditions, or directly through blood and sweat on the battlefield. Whatever path brought you to your mastery of historically proven tactics, you choose a tactical doctrine from the following options, each of which grants you a skill. (Quick Build: Vanguard and the Intimidate skill.)

Your tactical doctrine is your subclass, and your choice of doctrine determines many of the features you’ll gain as you gain new levels.

QbsiJwC2zZCfmQej.name: Focus
QbsiJwC2zZCfmQej.description: The ring of steel panics others but brings order to your mind, granting you a Heroic Resource called focus.

7k50JsLsnWjsjUNC.name: Field Arsenal
7k50JsLsnWjsjUNC.description: You have drilled with a broad array of weapons and have developed techniques to optimize their use. Whenever you select or change your kit, you can select an additional kit and gain the benefits of both kits, including both their signature abilities. See Kits for more information. (Quick Build: Shining Armor, Sniper)

MP2iZkK0a0TBrk2f.name: Mark
MP2iZkK0a0TBrk2f.description: You know how to focus the attention of your allies as you push them toward victory.

v3lv8gpmAuGMFZeL.name: 3 Focus
v3lv8gpmAuGMFZeL.description: Choose one heroic ability from the following options, each of which costs 3 focus to use. (Quick Build: Inspiring Strike.)

gxX5Ov4NXyFqjz7Y.name: 5 Focus
gxX5Ov4NXyFqjz7Y.description: Choose one heroic ability from the following options, each of which costs 5 focus to use. (Quick Build: Hammer and Anvil.)

ypbZTcMPbIMvHBh4.description: You gain one exploration, interpersonal, or intrigue perk of your choice.

o9nuiB0R6xyDLyeq.name: 7 Focus Ability
o9nuiB0R6xyDLyeq.description: Choose one heroic ability from the following options, each of which costs 7 focus to use.

xNHyFFc7jN1Stbxe.description: Your Might and Reason scores each increase to 3.

mbh82wJVgbnvv8or.description: Each of your characteristic scores increases by 1, to a maximum of 4.

zYEInq4FXtwupYWN.description: Your Might and Reason scores each increase to 5.

OItEzNHHo2zLQHlw.description: Your expertise with weapons has grown to true mastery.

description.value:
You have drilled with a broad array of arms and armor, and have developed techniques to optimize their use. You can use and gain the benefits of two kits, including both their signature abilities. Whenever you would choose or change one kit, you can choose or change your second kit as well. See Chapter 6: Kits for more information. (Quick Build: Shining Armor, Sniper.)

If both kits grant you the same benefit, you take one or the other and can’t change your choice until you finish a respite. (This usually means taking the higher of two bonuses.)

For example, if you take the Shining Armor and Sniper kits, you gain the following benefits overall:

  • Stamina Bonus: +12 per echelon

  • Stability Bonus: +1

  • Melee Damage Bonus: +2/+2/+2

  • Ranged Damage Bonus: +0/+0/+4

  • Speed Bonus: +1

  • Ranged Distance Bonus: +10

  • Disengage Bonus: +1

  • You can use the Patient Shot and Protective Attack signature abilities.

Kit signature abilities have their kit’s bonuses already applied, which might require you to adjust the bonuses of the signature abilities you gain from a kit. For example, you might take the Martial Artist kit, which gives a melee weapon damage bonus of +2/+2/+2, and the Mountain kit, which gives a melee weapon damage bonus of +0/+0/+4. If you choose to use the Mountain kit’s damage bonus, then the Battle Grace signature ability from the Martial Artist kit loses the +2/+2/+2 bonus from that kit, reducing its usual 5/8/11 damage for its tier 1, tier 2, and tier 3 outcomes to 3/6/9. It then gains the +0/+0/+4 of the Mountain kit to deal 3/6/13 damage.

Who says lightly armored heroes can’t also be hard to move? You just need to employ some psionics! The Battlemind kit harnesses the power of your mind to make you harder to move—and to make your foes easier to push around.

system.story: Your weapon unleashes psionic energy that reduces your target’s weight.

effect.after: Until the end of the target’s next turn, any forced movement that affects the target has its distance increased by 2.

description.value: The Warrior Priest kit imbues the power of the gods into your weapon, making it a smiting instrument. You wade into the fray without fear, thanks to the power of the divine … and the heavy armor you wear

system.story: The impact of your weapon brands your target for destruction

effect.after: Until the end of the target’s next turn, they have damage weakness equal to [[lookup @chr]]{the characteristic score used for this ability’s power roll}.

description.value:
The ring of steel panics others but brings order to your mind, granting you a Heroic Resource called focus.

Focus in Combat

At the start of a combat encounter or some other stressful situation tracked in combat rounds (as determined by the Director), you gain focus equal to your Victories. At the start of each of your turns during combat, you gain 2 focus.

Additionally, the first time each combat round that you or any ally damages a creature marked by you (see @UUID[Compendium.draw-steel.abilities.Item.zyd6C7phOZr6EAQ9]{Mark}), you gain 1 focus. The first time in a combat round that any ally within 10 squares of you uses a heroic ability, you gain 1 focus.

You lose any remaining focus at the end of the encounter.

Focus Outside of Combat

Though you can’t gain focus outside of combat, you can use your heroic abilities and effects that cost focus without spending it. Whenever you use an ability or effect outside of combat that costs focus, you can’t use that same ability or effect outside of combat again until you earn 1 or more Victories or finish a respite.

When you use an ability outside of combat that lets you spend unlimited focus on its effect, you can use it as if you had spent an amount of focus equal to your Victories.

system.story: You draw your allies’ attention to a specific foe—with devastating effect.

effect.before:
The target is [[/apply YW2JEdoDPd0XlaII]]{marked} by you until the end of the encounter, until you are dying, or until you use this ability again. You can willingly end your mark on a creature (no action required), and if another tactician marks a creature, your mark on that creature ends. When a creature marked by you is reduced to 0 Stamina, you can use a free triggered action to mark a new target within distance.

You can initially mark only one creature using this ability, though other tactician abilities allow you to mark additional creatures at the same time. The mastermind tactical doctrine’s Anticipation feature allows you to target additional creatures with this ability starting at 5th level.

While a creature marked by you is within your line of effect, you and allies within your line of effect gain an edge on power rolls made against that creature. Additionally, whenever you or any ally uses an ability to deal rolled damage to a creature marked by you, you can spend 1 focus to gain one of the following benefits as a free triggered action:

  • The ability deals extra [[/damage 2*@R]]{damage} equal to twice your Reason score.

  • The creature dealing the damage can spend a Recovery.

  • The creature dealing the damage can shift up to a number of squares equal to [[lookup @R]]{your Reason score}.

  • If you damage a creature marked by you with a melee ability, the creature is [[/apply taunted turn]] by you until the end of their next turn.

You can’t gain more than one benefit from the same trigger

description.value: Whenever you speak one-on-one with a creature, you can ask them one question about themself that might typically offend them or raise suspicion. If they choose not to answer honestly, they simply deflect or redirect the question, with no further complications. If they choose to answer honestly, the creature can immediately ask you a question about yourself in turn, which you must answer honestly.

system.story: Your foe left an opening. You point this out to an ally!

effect.before: The target can use a signature ability as a free triggered action.

spend.text: You target two allies instead of one.

description.value: You have learned the stratagems of ancient heroes, letting you lead from the front lines and seek victory through sheer force of will and personality. You gain a skill from the interpersonal skill group.

GK5Xal3qdQKHwNvz.name: Melee Superiority
GK5Xal3qdQKHwNvz.description: Your tactical doctrine grants you the following feature.

qAYqu1eZObtNYmVr.name: 5th-Level Vanguard Features
qAYqu1eZObtNYmVr.description: Your tactical doctrine grants you two features, as shown on the 5th-Level Doctrine Features table.

5yrmJ6f9ytlGnsRo.name: 6th-Level Vanguard Ability
5yrmJ6f9ytlGnsRo.description: Your tactical doctrine grants your choice of one of two heroic abilities.

uvqx6hiGMQcxPCey.name: 8th-Level Vanguard Feature
uvqx6hiGMQcxPCey.description: Your tactical doctrine grants you a feature

RRiBJ5jWYcSpFqeD.name: 7th-Level Vanguard Feature
RRiBJ5jWYcSpFqeD.description: Your tactical doctrine grants you a feature

50Ss8Xh8svc2ppfs.name: 9th-Level Vanguard Ability
50Ss8Xh8svc2ppfs.description: Your tactical doctrine grants your choice of one of two heroic abilities.

description.value: You command any room you walk into. While you are present during a negotiation, each hero with you treats their Renown as 2 higher than usual. Additionally, each hero with you during a combat encounter has a double edge on tests made to stop combat and start a negotiation.

After constant drills, you can more accurately anticipate an enemy’s plan and thwart their attempts to move across the battlefield. Whenever you make an opportunity attack, the target’s speed is reduced to 0 until the end of the current turn.

Mark Benefit: When a creature marked by you attempts to move or shift within distance of your melee free strike, you can use a free triggered action and spend 2 focus to make a melee free strike against that creature

system.story: You prioritize saving an ally over your own safety.
system.trigger: The target deals damage to an ally.

tier1.display: {{potency}}, the target is frightened of the triggering ally (save ends)

effect.before: You move up to your speed toward the triggering ally, ending this movement adjacent to them or in the nearest square if you can’t reach an adjacent square. The triggering ally can spend a Recovery and gains [[/heal 5 temporary]] for each enemy you came adjacent to during the move. You then make a power roll against the target.

system.story: Your quick reflexes cost an enemy the precision they seek.
system.trigger: A creature deals damage to the target.

effect.before: You can shift 1 square. If the target is you, or if you end this shift adjacent to the target, the target takes half the damage. If the damage has any potency effect associated with it, the potency is decreased by 1.

spend.text: This ability’s distance becomes Melee 1 + your  Reason score, and you can shift up to a number of squares equal to  your Reason score instead of 1 square.

system.story: Your precise strike leaves your foe struggling to respond.
Your attack demoralizes your foe. Your allies begin to think you can win.

effect.after: The first time any ally deals damage any target you’ve marked before the start of your next turn, that ally can spend a Recovery.

effect.before: Choose an enemy adjacent to you. The next ability power roll an ally who makes against that creature before the start of your next turn has an edge.

A creature who uses the Catch Breath maneuver spends a Recovery and regains [[/heal @recoveries.recoveryValue]]{Stamina} equal to their recovery value.

A creature who is dying can’t use the Catch Breath maneuver, but other creatures can help them spend Recoveries in other ways.

When a creature takes the Charge main action, they move up to their speed in a straight line without jumping, then make a @UUID[Compendium.draw-steel.abilities.Item.wU69Y06G9lYFrvp6]{melee free strike} against a target when they end their move. If the creature has an ability with the Charge keyword, they can use that ability against the target instead of a free strike.

A creature can’t move through difficult terrain or shift when they charge. They can fly or burrow as part of the Charge main action if they have that movement available to them, but they can’t climb or swim while charging unless they can automatically use that movement at full speed.

effects.name: Defending
effects.description: When a creature takes the Defend main action, ability rolls made against them have a double bane until the start of their next turn. Additionally, you have a double edge on tests when called for to resist environmental effects or a creature’s traits or abilities. A creature gains no benefit from this action while another creature is taunted by them.

effect.before: When a creature takes the [[/apply TYggdI2Kwa302NjW]]{Defend main action}, ability rolls made against them have a double bane until the start of their next turn. Additionally, you have a double edge on tests when called for to resist environmental effects or a creature’s traits or abilities. A creature gains no benefit from this action while another creature is taunted by them.
A creature who is grabbed by another creature, an object, or an effect (see Grab below) can attempt to escape by using the following ability.
effect.after: You take a bane on this maneuver if your size is smaller than the size of the creature, object, or effect that has you grabbed.

tier2.display: You can grab the target, but if you do, the target can make a melee free strike against you before they are grabbed

tier3.display: The target is grabbed by you.

effect.before: A creature seeking to keep a foe close and locked down can attempt to grab a creature using the following ability
effect.after: You can usually target only creatures of your size or smaller. If your Might score is 2 or higher, you can target any creature with a size equal to or less than your Might score.

effect.before: A creature who uses the Heal main action employs medicine or inspiring words to make an adjacent creature feel better and stay in the fight. The target creature can spend a Recovery to regain Stamina, or can make a saving throw against one effect they are suffering that is ended by a saving throw.

effect.after: You can usually target only creatures of your size or smaller. If your Might score is 2 or higher, you can target any creature with a size equal to or less than your Might score.

effect.before: A creature can use the Stand Up maneuver to stand up if they are prone, ending that condition. Alternatively, they can use this maneuver to make a willing adjacent prone creature stand up.
When a creature takes the Advance move action, they move a number of squares up to their speed. They can break up this movement with their maneuver and main action however they wish.

When a creature takes the Disengage move action, they can shift [[lookup @movement.disengage]]{1} square(s). Certain class features, kits, and other rules allow a creature to shift more than 1 square when they disengage. A creature who does so can break up their shift with their maneuver and main action however they wish.

A creature can take the Ride move action only while mounted on another creature. When a creature takes the Ride move action, they cause their mount to move up to the mount’s speed, taking the rider with them. Alternatively, a creature can use the Ride move action to have their mount use the Disengage move action as a free triggered action. A creature can use the Ride move action only once per round. A mounted creature can only have this move action applied to them once per round. This movement can be broken up with the rider’s maneuver and main action however they wish.

biography.value:
Warden-Sergeant Eirik Greyhand commands the Chainwarden Toll at Greypass—a fortified checkpoint in the Midgard's Shield foothills. He is not corrupt, just exhausted. The toll is underfunded, understaffed, and overwhelmed by refugees.

Eirik wants order. He wants the fiction that the system works. He desperately needs supplies—fuel and rations are worth more than coin. He has a family in Hrafnborg and hasn't heard from them in a month; any news is worth something to him.
biography.director:
Eirik is the Beat 4 negotiation encounter at the Greypass Toll. He is not an antagonist—he's a cog in a machine that's breaking down.

Negotiation approaches:

  • Present forged papers (Presence): Tier 2+: satisfied. Tier 1: notices seal is wrong—+1 Interest (suspicious but curious).
  • Offer rations or fuel: Tier 2+: earns 2 Interest immediately, waves them through, offers road intelligence.
  • Share news of Hrafnborg (Presence): Tier 2+: visible relief, +1 Interest.
  • Bluff military connection (Presence): Tier 3 only: buys it. Tier 1–2: he knows every Warden—−1 Patience.
  • Threaten or intimidate: Catastrophic. 8 wardens and fortified position. −2 Patience.
  • Present Bryn's toll-token: One party member passes free. Asks after Bryn.

In Beat 8, the toll-post is dark on the return journey. Eirik has evacuated south. A note on the door reads: 'Recalled to Hrafnborg. Orders of the King.'—illustrating the system collapsing inward.
